old_claim.note: Construction provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Hydroelectric generation is one explicitly recognized renewable source integrated through the grid framework, while the portfolio does not require hydro at every site.

source_shape.source_locator: FERC's 'What is a Qualifying Facility?' section defines eligible small power production by renewable primary sources including hydro, wind, solar, biomass, waste, and geothermal resources.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The 1978 grid-integration framework names hydropower as a participating renewable-generation class rather than treating generic construction as a technology-specific generation input.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Hydroelectric power is a direct member of the scoped renewable portfolio. Classical monumental construction is remote, generic infrastructure ancestry and does not distinguish renewable grid integration from nearly every other built system.

ontology_before: The renewable-integration node named hydro in its description but omitted the graph's hydroelectric-generation node, while directly depending on generic classical construction.
ontology_after: The node includes hydroelectric generation as one enabling renewable source and removes generic construction from its direct technology boundary.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed and locked by graph invariant 2026-07-16-renewable-grid-integration-scope: hydroelectric_power_plants is present and construction is absent.
would_reject_if: A reliable source shows that hydroelectric generation is excluded from the scoped renewable grid framework.
The node is broadened from renewable power grid integration to construction of all energy infrastructure.
short_reason: The governing framework explicitly includes hydro, while generic construction is non-specific background rather than a direct renewable-integration prerequisite.
